pages.raw_text:
All trains on the main line of the
o. r. & n. are this morning an
nulled as the result of Monday
nights Chinook wind and yester-
days rising temperature. The
greatest trouble is reported in
Hays canyon. Pendleton is entire
ly cut off from traffic and many of
the streets are under water. The
Umatilla river has swollen till it is
nearly as high as it was at any time
last week, when it reached the
high water mark. All the new
roadbed of the 0. r. & n. at
Havana has been destroyed by the
overflow of Wild Horse creek.


No traffic on the Northern Paci-
fic can proceed further west on the
main line than Ritzville, as a
washout has occurred near that
place. Trains from this line are
being run over the Great Northern
line west today. No washouts are
yet reported on the Great Northern
today. No. l from the east is seven
hours late, while trains coming in
from the west are only about an
hour behind time. Heavy trains of
freight have been pulled over this
road for the past few days, and 1t
Is reported that nearly all that had
been delayed have been released.
